title: A Kinder, Gentler Larry Ellison? Oracle Presents New Face to Its User Community
author: Katherine Jones
source_file: 2002 A Kinder, Gentler Larry Ellison_ Oracle Presents New Face to Its User Community.pdf
abstract: This Aberdeen InSight, published May 2002, reviews the Oracle Apps World conference held in San Diego. The study observes a notable shift in Oracle's posture toward its installed base: fewer new product announcements, heightened emphasis on customer satisfaction, and a strategic focus on migrating customers to the web-based e-Business Suite (Oracle 11i). The piece contextualizes this shift within the stark realities of the post-dot-com enterprise software market.
importance_rationale: Documents Oracle's strategic pivot to installed-base retention at a critical post-dot-com moment; reflects broader ERP industry dynamics that shaped vendor strategies for a decade.
relevance_rationale: Oracle's recurring challenge of migrating customers from legacy versions to newer platforms is perennially relevant; the installed-base monetization strategy described here remains a core SaaS/ERP industry dynamic.
prescience_rationale: The prediction that Oracle would focus on installed-base upgrades as its primary revenue opportunity proved correct; Oracle 11i support extended through 2013+ and the company successfully transitioned to cloud ERP (Oracle Fusion) over the subsequent decade.
